text: Cal Newport argues that deep work requires more than willpower — it requires architecture. The ritual is the architecture. Newport studied the routines of figures like Carl Jung, who built a stone tower at Bollingen specifically to think without interruption, and Donald Knuth, who disconnected from email entirely in 1990. Both recognized that sustained cognitive effort cannot be summoned on demand; it must be coaxed through consistent environmental and behavioral cues. A fixed start time, a particular location, and a defined ending condition train the brain to enter a focused state with less friction each time the ritual begins.
question: According to the passage, why did Carl Jung build a tower at Bollingen?

options.text: To store his personal library and manuscripts away from visitors
To think without interruption and support sustained cognitive effort
To demonstrate that physical isolation improves academic output
To follow a trend among European intellectuals of his era
